q.en: At the start of Chapter 12, what do the 'dark waves of men' sweeping from the woods mean?
q.zh: 第12章开头，从树林里涌出的“黑色的人浪”意味着什么？

options.en: The fight was lost and the men were fleeing
options.zh: 战斗败了，那些人在溃逃

options.en: The general was giving a long speech
options.zh: 将军正在发表长篇演讲

options.en: A large picnic was being set up near the trees
options.zh: 有人在树边摆一场大野餐

options.en: A band had started to play music

explain.en: The text says the fight was lost and the dark waves charged down upon Henry.
explain.zh: 原文说战斗已经输了，那黑色的人浪朝亨利冲了下来。

q.en: When Henry grabs a fleeing man by the arm, what happens?
q.zh: 亨利抓住一个逃跑者的胳膊时，发生了什么？

options.en: They sit and share some food
options.zh: 他们坐下分吃了点东西

options.en: They shake hands and walk off together according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们握手，一起走开了（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The man swings his rifle and hits Henry on the head
options.zh: 那人抡起步枪砸中了亨利的头

options.en: The man gives Henry his canteen

explain.en: The man bawls 'Let go me!' and adroitly swings his rifle, crushing Henry's head.
explain.zh: 那人大喊“放开我！”，灵巧地抡起步枪，砸在亨利头上。

q.en: The man swings his rifle 'adroitly'. What does adroitly mean?
q.zh: 那人“adroitly”抡起步枪。adroitly 是什么意思？

options.en: In a slow and sleepy way
Skillfully or cleverly
In a clumsy, awkward manner
With a very loud angry shout
options.zh: 伴随着很大、很怒的喊声

explain.en: Adroitly is defined as skillfully or cleverly.
explain.zh: adroitly 的解释是“熟练而机灵地”。

q.en: What hurt does Henry receive in this part of the story?
q.zh: 在这一部分里，亨利受了什么伤？

options.en: A scrape across his back
A cut on his head from the rifle blow
options.zh: 步枪砸出的、头上的一道伤口

options.en: A burn upon his right hand
A deep wound upon his left foot according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 左脚上一道很深的伤口（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The rifle crushes Henry's head; later it is called a graze from a ball—his red badge.
explain.zh: 步枪砸中了亨利的头；后文称之为子弹擦过——正是他的“红色勋章”。

q.en: How does Henry feel when the enemy pours out and the battle is lost?
q.zh: 当敌人涌出、战斗已输时，亨利是什么感受？

options.en: He is happy the war is over
He is terribly bored and only wants to fall asleep
options.zh: 他无聊得要命，只想倒头大睡

options.en: He is hungry for some food
He feels horror and great amazement
options.zh: 他感到恐惧与极大的惊愕

explain.en: The book says Henry was horrorstricken; he stared in agony and amazement.
explain.zh: 书里说亨利“惊恐万状”，他既痛苦又惊愕地盯着看。

q.en: Who helps Henry after he is hurt in Chapter 12?
q.zh: 第12章亨利受伤后，是谁帮了他？

options.en: A small boy who is lost
His old friend from the river camp according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他在河边营地时的老朋友（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The general of the division
A cheery soldier he has never met before
options.zh: 一名素未谋面、乐呵呵的士兵

explain.en: A cheery-voiced man takes Henry's arm and leads him toward his regiment.
explain.zh: 一个声音乐呵呵的人扶住亨利的胳膊，把他领向自己的团。

q.en: After the blow, Henry's face shows 'pallor'. What does pallor mean?
q.zh: 挨了一下后，亨利的脸显出“pallor”。pallor 是什么意思？

options.en: A look of deep, calm sleep
A broad, happy smile
Pale; a lack of normal color
A bright, angry red glowing color

explain.en: Pallor is defined as a lack of normal color in the face.
explain.zh: pallor 的解释是“脸上缺少正常的血色”，即苍白。

q.en: At the end, what does Henry realize about the cheery man?
q.zh: 结尾时，亨利对那个乐呵呵的士兵有了什么发现？

options.en: They become the very best of life-long close friends
options.zh: 他们成了终生的、最要好的朋友

options.en: He never once saw the man's face
options.zh: 他自始至终都没看清那人的脸

options.en: The man had known Henry as a small child
options.zh: 那人早就认识小时候的亨利

options.en: The man was really the regiment's colonel

explain.en: As the man strides away, Henry notices he had not once seen his face.
explain.zh: 那人大步走开时，亨利才察觉自己竟从没看清过他的脸。

q.en: Why might the author include a kind stranger who helps Henry and then leaves?
q.zh: 作者为什么要安排一个好心的陌生人帮了亨利、然后又离开？

options.en: To show kindness can come from strangers
options.zh: 为了表现善意也会来自陌生人

options.en: To list the names of all the generals
options.zh: 为了列出所有将军的名字

options.en: To teach a lesson about riding horses
To give a step-by-step lesson in forest cooking
options.zh: 为了一步步教人如何在林中做饭

explain.en: The stranger's quiet help shows that comfort can appear from unknown people in hard times.
explain.zh: 陌生人无声的帮助，表现了在艰难时刻，慰藉也可能来自素不相识的人。

q.en: What does the cheery man first say when he speaks to Henry?
q.zh: 那个乐呵呵的人开口对亨利说的第一句话是什么？

options.en: 'Yeh seem in a bad way, boy?'
'Where is your rifle, soldier?'
'Hurry and get into the marching line right now!'
'Have you seen my lost hat?'

explain.en: The text quotes him: 'Yeh seem t' be in a pretty bad way, boy?'
explain.zh: 原文引用他的话：“你看起来不大妙啊，小伙子？”

prompt.en: A stranger helps Henry and then disappears without Henry ever seeing his face. Have you ever been helped by someone you did not know? Why do you think small kind acts matter?
prompt.zh: 一个陌生人帮了亨利，然后就消失了，亨利连他的脸都没看清。你有没有被不认识的人帮过？你觉得小小的善举为什么重要？

en: Recall that Henry was alone, hurt, and unsure where to go.
The stranger gave help with no reward and asked for nothing.
Think how a small act can change someone's whole day.
zh: 回想亨利当时孤身一人、受了伤、不知该往哪走。
陌生人帮了忙却不要回报，也没提任何要求。
想想一个小小的举动如何能改变一个人一整天的状态。
